# Repository Labels

This document lists the recommended GitHub labels for the **Open Source Contribution Atelier** project. These labels help categorize issues, manage workflow states, and assist contributors in finding appropriate tasks (specifically for Social Summer of Code).

## Recommended Labels Reference

| Label Name | Hex Color | Description | Usage |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| `SSOC` | `#FF9D3D` | Official tag for all Social Summer of Code Season 5 items | Add to all active SSOC challenges and curriculum tracks. |
| `ssoc:good-first-issue` | `#50FA7B` | Extremely beginner-friendly task for first-time contributors | For documentation typos, small React CSS tweaks, or simple CLI outputs. |
| `ssoc:mentor-needed` | `#8BE9FD` | Requires support, explanation, or guidance from a mentor | Use when code changes require high-level design approval or sandbox knowledge. |
| `bug` | `#FF5555` | Outlines unexpected behavior or reproducible platform errors | Default for verified bug reports. |
| `enhancement` | `#BD93F9` | New features, curriculum additions, or visual upgrades | For feature requests or UX improvements. |
| `curriculum` | `#F1FA8C` | Platform lessons, interactive exercises, or instructions | For lesson improvements or adding new interactive bash exercises. |
| `needs-triage` | `#6C7086` | New issue requiring validation, category tags, or priority | Automatically added to new issues by templates. |
| `blocked` | `#FF79C6` | Blocked by another active PR, upstream dependency, or design decision | Used to signal work is temporarily on hold. |

## Label Automation in Templates

Issue templates in `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` automatically apply the appropriate labels upon creation. This guarantees that issues stay organized from day one.
